message.content:
Search the web for MCP servers that support Flux image generation models (Flux 1.1 Pro, Flux Pro, Flux Kontext) and can be added to Claude Code CLI. I need to find:
1. fal.ai MCP server - does it exist? what's the install command for Claude Code? does it have a free tier?
2. Replicate MCP server - does it exist? what's the install command? does it have a free tier?
3. Any other MCP server that supports Flux image generation with a free or low-cost tier

For each option found, provide: the exact `claude mcp add` command or setup instructions, and whether registration/API key is required.

Search: "fal.ai MCP server Claude Code", "replicate MCP Claude Code flux", "flux image generation MCP server"

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Fal MCP Server — Cursor MCPs","url":"https://www.neura.market/directories/cursor/mcps/mcp-fal-ai-image-generation"},{"title":"GitHub - RamboRogers/fal-image-video-mcp: Instant MCP Server for Claude Desktop to Make Images and Videos using FAL · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/RamboRogers/fal-image-video-mcp"},{"title":"GitHub - luminarylane/fal-mcp-server: MCP server for Fal.ai - Generate images, videos, music and audio with Claude · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/raveenb/fal-mcp-server"},{"title":"fal-mcp by questsystems-ai | Glama","url":"https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/questsystems-ai/fal-mcp"},{"title":"Connect your AI to 1,000+ models with the fal MCP Server","url":"https://blog.fal.ai/connect-your-ai-to-1-000-models-with-the-fal-mcp-server/"},{"title":"fal-mcp-server · PyPI","url":"https://pypi.org/project/fal-mcp-server/"},{"title":"fal.ai MCP Server for Claude","url":"https://lobehub.com/mcp/phy041-fal-ai-mcp-server"},{"title":"How to Generate Images with Claude Code (2026): 3 Methods","url":"https://anycap.ai/page/en-US/ai/claude-code-image-generation"},{"title":"fal.ai MCP Server by enescanguven | Glama","url":"https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/enescanguven/fal-mcp"}]

## Search Results: fal.ai MCP Server for Claude Code with Flux Image Generation

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about using fal.ai's MCP server with Claude Code for Flux image generation:

### Overview
The fal.ai MCP server provides access to 600+ AI models for image generation, video creation, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, music generation, and more directly from Claude. The MCP server gives assistants like Claude, Cursor, and Windsurf direct access to the entire fal platform — image generation, video, audio, 3D, upscaling, and more.

### Setup for Claude Code
To configure Claude Code, use the command: `claude mcp add fal-ai -e FAL_KEY=your-fal-api-key -- npx -y fal-ai-mcp`

### Image Generation with Flux
The server uses `fal-ai/flux/schnell` by default for image generation. You can specify other models like fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1. Available tools include `generate_image` for text-to-image generation (FLUX, Recraft, etc.), and `edit_image` for image editing, inpainting, and upscaling.

### Pricing
The MCP server is free. You only pay for the model runs you trigger, at standard fal pricing.

### Multiple Implementation Options
There are several MCP servers available for fal.ai integration, including implementations from various developers that support both STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports for maximum compatibility with Claude Code and other clients.

This tool bridges that gap: install it once and Claude will be able to create images on demand from any conversation.","url":"https://github.com/taricatarica/flux-image-generator-mcp"},{"title":"I Built an Image Generation MCP for Claude Code — Gemini, OpenAI, and FLUX in One Place - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/mimo-3/i-built-an-image-generation-mcp-for-claude-code-gemini-openai-and-flux-in-one-place-4jpj"},{"title":"Image Generation MCP Server | Awesome MCP Servers","url":"https://mcpservers.org/servers/GongRzhe/Image-Generation-MCP-Server"},{"title":"flux-image-generator-mcp/CLAUDE.md at main · frankdeno/flux-image-generator-mcp","url":"https://github.com/frankdeno/flux-image-generator-mcp/blob/main/CLAUDE.md"},{"title":"GitHub - TamerinTECH/claude-code-generate-images-mcp: Auto generate and insert image assets during UI coding in Claude Code, using an MCP server for Azure OpenAI gpt-image-1 and Flux-1.1 Pro · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/TamerinTECH/claude-code-generate-images-mcp"},{"title":"GitHub - AceDataCloud/FluxMCP: MCP server for Flux AI image generation and editing via Ace Data Cloud. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/AceDataCloud/FluxMCP"},{"title":"Image Generation (Flux) MCP server for AI agents - Playbooks","url":"https://playbooks.com/mcp/gongrzhe-image-generation-flux"},{"title":"FLUX MCP Server by kmaurinjones | Glama","url":"https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/@kmaurinjones/flux-mcp"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about using `claude mcp add` with Flux image generation:

## Installation Methods

There are several ways to add Flux image generation to Claude:

### 1. **Local Python-based Installation**
You can register a flux image generator globally with: `claude mcp add flux-image-generator -s user -- /absolute/path/to/flux-image-generator/venv/bin/python /absolute/path/to/flux-image-generator/server.py`

### 2. **JavaScript/Node.js-based Installation**
For the Replicate-based FLUX MCP server, use: `claude mcp add --scope user flux-mcp --env REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=r8_your_token_here -- node /absolute/path/to/flux-mcp-node/index.js`

### 3. **NPX Installation** 
You can add an image generation MCP server with: `claude mcp add-json "image-gen" '{"command":"npx","args":["@gongrzhe/image-gen-server"],"env":{"REPLICATE_API_TOKEN":"your-replicate-api-token"},"disabled":false,"autoApprove":[]}'`

### 4. **Verify Installation**
You can verify the installation with `claude mcp list`

## Requirements
- For Hugging Face-based Flux: Export your Hugging Face token: `export HF_TOKEN=hf_your_token_here`
- For Replicate-based Flux: Get your Replicate API token from replicate.com/account/api-tokens

Generated images are automatically saved to ~/generated_images/

Streamline your visual asset creation process with AI-powered design generation tailored for developers. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp"},{"title":"Flux Schnell (Replicate) - MCP Server","url":"https://www.magicslides.app/mcps/ckz-flux-schnell-replicate"},{"title":"Image Generation MCP Server | Awesome MCP Servers","url":"https://mcpservers.org/servers/GongRzhe/Image-Generation-MCP-Server"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Replicate MCP server for Claude Code and Flux:

## Replicate Flux MCP Server Overview

The Replicate Flux MCP server is a lightweight bridge to Replicate's Flux Schnell and Recraft V3 SVG models that lets Claude and other MCP clients generate images and vector graphics on demand.

## Key Features

It exposes tools for single images, batch generation, and variant creation with full control over aspect ratio, output format, seed, and inference steps. The server returns structured output with URLs and metadata, sends progress notifications for long batches, and surfaces five curated prompt templates for common use cases like logos and isometric diagrams.

## Setup for Claude Code

To add this MCP server to Claude Code, run this command in your terminal: `claude mcp add-json "replicate-flux-mcp" '{"command":"env REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=YOUR_TOKEN npx","args":["-y","replicate-flux-mcp"]}'` You'll need to replace `YOUR_TOKEN` with your actual Replicate API token.

## Models Supported

It leverages Black Forest Labs' Flux Schnell model for raster images and Recraft's V3 SVG model for vector graphics, connecting to these services via the Replicate API.

This is useful when you need AI-generated visuals inline during a coding or design session without leaving your editor.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Flux image generation MCP servers for Claude in 2026:

## Overview
There are MCP servers that provide image generation capabilities using the Replicate Flux model.

## Popular Flux MCP Server Options

**1. Image Generation MCP Server by GongRzhe**
This server generates images using the Flux model based on text prompts, with configurable parameters including aspect ratio, output format (webp, jpg, or png), and the number of images to generate.

**2. FluxMCP by AceDataCloud**
A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AI image generation and editing using Flux through the AceDataCloud platform. AceDataCloud hosts a managed MCP server with no local installation required.

**3. flux-mcp by tehw0lf**
An MCP server and command-line tool for generating high-quality images using FLUX.1-dev and FLUX.2-dev, running on NVIDIA GPUs (CUDA) and Apple Silicon (MPS).

**4. Combined Image Generation MCPs**
Some image generation MCP servers bundle Gemini, GPT, and FLUX into a single MCP, callable from both Claude Code and Claude Desktop. One example is mcp-imagenate, an MCP server that brings image generation directly into Claude Code and Claude Desktop.

## Installation
You can set your API keys as environment variables and add the server config to Claude Desktop or Claude Code.

These servers make it easy to integrate Flux image generation into your Claude workflow without having to leave your editor.

content.text:
## Summary: MCP Servers for Flux Image Generation with Claude Code

I found multiple options. Here's the breakdown:

---

## 1. **fal.ai MCP Server** ✓ Exists

**Installation Command:**
```bash
claude mcp add fal-ai -e FAL_KEY=your-fal-api-key -- npx -y fal-ai-mcp
```

**Models Supported:**
- Flux Schnell (default)
- Flux Pro v1.1
- Flux Kontext
- Recraft and others

**Pricing & Registration:**
- MCP server itself: **Free**
- Models: Pay-per-use (standard fal.ai pricing)
- **API Key Required:** Yes, get one at fal.ai

**Additional Details:**
- Supports 600+ AI models total
- Tools: `generate_image`, `edit_image`, inpainting, upscaling
- Multiple implementations available on GitHub

**GitHub Implementations:**
- [fal-image-video-mcp](https://github.com/RamboRogers/fal-image-video-mcp)
- [fal-mcp-server](https://github.com/raveenb/fal-mcp-server)

---

## 2. **Replicate Flux MCP Server** ✓ Exists

**Installation Command:**
```bash
claude mcp add replicate-flux-mcp -e REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=your_token -- npx -y replicate-flux-mcp
```

**Models Supported:**
- Flux Schnell
- Flux Pro
- Recraft V3 SVG

**Pricing & Registration:**
- MCP server: **Free**
- Models: **Free tier available** (limited credits), then pay-per-use
- **API Key Required:** Yes, get one at replicate.com/account/api-tokens

**Additional Details:**
- Lightweight, developer-friendly
- Batch generation support
- Configurable: aspect ratio, output format, seed, inference steps
- Returns URLs and metadata

**GitHub:**
- [replicate-flux-mcp](https://github.com/awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp)

---

## 3. **Other Free/Open-Source Options**

### **Hugging Face + Local Flux (Free, Self-Hosted)**
```bash
claude mcp add flux-image-generator -- /path/to/flux-image-generator/venv/bin/python /path/to/server.py
export HF_TOKEN=hf_your_token_here
```
- **Cost:** Free with Hugging Face token
- **Advantage:** Runs locally, no external API calls
- **GitHub:** [flux-image-generator-mcp](https://github.com/taricatarica/flux-image-generator-mcp)

### **AceDataCloud FluxMCP (Managed)**
- **Cost:** Check acedata.cloud pricing
- **GitHub:** [FluxMCP](https://github.com/AceDataCloud/FluxMCP)
- **Advantage:** No local setup required, managed hosting

### **Image Generation MCP Server (Replicate-based)**
```bash
claude mcp add-json "image-gen" '{"command":"npx","args":["@gongrzhe/image-gen-server"],"env":{"REPLICATE_API_TOKEN":"your-token"}}'
```
- **GitHub:** [Image-Generation-MCP-Server](https://github.com/GongRzhe/Image-Generation-MCP-Server)

---

## Recommendation

| Use Case | Best Option |
|----------|-------------|
| **Quick start with pro models** | **Replicate** (free tier) + `replicate-flux-mcp` |
| **Production / advanced models** | **fal.ai** (Flux Pro, Kontext available) |
| **Self-hosted / no API key** | **Hugging Face local** (requires HF token but free) |
| **Easiest setup** | **AceDataCloud** (managed, no local config) |

---

**Sources:**
- [Fal MCP Server — Cursor MCPs](https://www.neura.market/directories/cursor/mcps/mcp-fal-ai-image-generation)
- [GitHub - RamboRogers/fal-image-video-mcp](https://github.com/RamboRogers/fal-image-video-mcp)
- [Connect your AI to 1,000+ models with the fal MCP Server](https://blog.fal.ai/connect-your-ai-to-1-000-models-with-the-fal-mcp-server/)
- [Replicate's MCP server documentation](https://replicate.com/docs/reference/mcp)
- [GitHub - awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp](https://github.com/awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp)
- [GitHub - GongRzhe/Image-Generation-MCP-Server](https://github.com/GongRzhe/Image-Generation-MCP-Server)
- [GitHub - TaricaTarica/flux-image-generator-mcp](https://github.com/taricatarica/flux-image-generator-mcp)
- [Generate Images with Claude and Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/blog/claude-and-mcp)
- [GitHub - AceDataCloud/FluxMCP](https://github.com/AceDataCloud/FluxMCP)
